title: Favorite song
wikitext:
[]: Favorite song?             Quote Post by terminus » Fri May 23, 2008 7:12 pm What's your favorite song in all of Evangelion? mine would have to be in EoE (Komm, süsser Tod) http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bCLoNOYcVQU and "Thanatos" http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vUYxiKs2Ozw when Rei sacrifices herself for Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri May 23, 2008 7:26 pm Komm Susser Todd has to be my favorite too. It relaxes me, then it makes me want to strangle someone I love.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Fri May 23, 2008 8:18 pm The Beast. And if this includes RoE, then I'd go for either Beautiful World or Les Betes.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Fri May 23, 2008 11:24 pm Komm Susser Tod for me. I love slide guitar in general, and its put to really good use in the song. Also, the dark, depressing theme is something I can identify with.

[]:             Quote Post by chazthesilencer » Sat May 24, 2008 2:05 am Do we know the name of the music played along to Eva01's switch to the dummy system? The piece is used three times in the series, episodes 16, 18 and 19. You hear it, and know something awesome and violent is about to happen.

[]:             Quote Post by Stacifer » Sat May 24, 2008 6:03 am Series Marking time, waiting for death Asuka strikes! Rei, Rei II Nerv Misato Eva00, The Beast I, Shinji Thanatos EoE End of Midsummer Substitute invasion Passage of emptiness Honeymoon with anxiety All of EoE is great, except for, in my opinion, Komm susser tod and Thanatos(if i can't be yours). With the latter, I think it's ruined with those vocals. I really don't like them at all!

[]:             Quote Post by zimlord » Sat May 24, 2008 9:06 am ANGELS OF DOOM ...and Final Showdown .

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat May 24, 2008 9:28 am Cruel Angel's Thesis! Or Everything You've Ever Dreamed I loved Komm when I first heard it, but I got burnt out.

[]:             Quote Post by Squirrel Ninja » Sat May 24, 2008 7:48 pm I really liked a lot of the music, but I'd give the top spot to Cannon in D.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat May 24, 2008 7:58 pm It relaxes me, then it makes me want to strangle someone I love.

[]:             Quote Post by Kurochi » Sat May 24, 2008 9:04 pm Squirrel Ninja wrote: I really liked a lot of the music, but I'd give the top spot to Cannon in D.

[]:             Quote Post by universalperson » Sat May 24, 2008 9:20 pm Kurochi wrote: Squirrel Ninja wrote: I really liked a lot of the music, but I'd give the top spot to Cannon in D.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sat May 24, 2008 10:54 pm My favorite is The Beast II, that song just kicks arse.

[]:             Quote Post by terminus » Sat May 24, 2008 11:54 pm Kurochi wrote: Squirrel Ninja wrote: I really liked a lot of the music, but I'd give the top spot to Cannon in D.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun May 25, 2008 12:40 am ............................Evangelion EATH.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun May 25, 2008 12:41 am terminus wrote: lol I loved that video, but when was there Canon in D in Evangelion?

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Sun May 25, 2008 1:19 am I couldn't pick just one: Komm Susser Tod Final Showdown Les Beast Angels of Doom Beautiful World Cruel Angel's Thesis All real life classical pieces (Hallelujah)

[]:             Quote Post by PartyHard » Sun May 25, 2008 7:16 am not counting instrumentals Flyyyyy me to the moon and let me plaaay among the sta-ars (was it ever released in the merchandise with Sinatra?) Also Cruel Angel's Thesis, Beautiful World.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun May 25, 2008 8:34 am Komm süsser Tod It was genious mix of (nigh-identical) chord progression nicked from Canon added together with hand clapping and melody from Hey Jude's nanananaa section juxtaposed with dark lyrics. It is genious song in all fronts. And like someone said, slide guitar is awesome Thanatos It's best piece from TV-series Expansion of Blockade (閉塞の拡大, Heisoku no Kakudai) It has bazillion names depending on source but this is the piece that starts to play after real world material in End of Evangelion and carries through Rei's death etc. It is best animusic I've ever heard. It is fantastic composition that shiftes dynamics all the time and mixes heartbreaking violin sections with booming brass horns with perfect grace. One of my favourite classic compositions and propably only such to rise from TV-show (well, Ailes Grises is another great one) Cruel Angel Thesis is great too

[]:             Quote Post by Nigoki » Thu May 29, 2008 10:32 pm Jesus Bleibet Meine Freude (Jesus, Joy of Man's Desiring) from the EoE OST.
